title: N-Queens
coreIdea: Place N queens on an NÃ—N chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Place queens row by row, backtracking whenever a placement violates column, diagonal, or anti-diagonal constraints.
recognitionCues: grid-based placement problem with mutual exclusion constraints
asks for all valid configurations or count of them
approach: Recurse row by row. For each row, try placing a queen in each column, checking if it's safe (no conflict with previously placed queens in column, upper-left diagonal, upper-right diagonal using sets or arrays for O(1) checks). If safe, place and recurse to next row; on return, backtrack by removing the queen.
spaceComplexity: O(N^2) for board, O(N) for tracking columns/diagonals
